description: You are the assessment coordinator for MATH201 Probability and Statistics. Build a randomized mid-term examination using Moodle's question bank. Populate the 'Probability Basics' category with three multiple-choice questions: Q1 ('What is the probability of rolling a prime number on a standard 6-sided die?' with correct answer '1/2' and distractors '1/3', '2/3', '1/6'), Q2 ('If events A and B are independent with P(A) = 0.4 and P(B) = 0.3, what is P(A and B)?' with correct answer '0.12' and distractors '0.70', '0.10', '0.34'), and Q3 ('A standard deck has 52 cards. What is the probability of drawing a King?' with correct answer '1/13' and distractors '4/52', '1/4', '1/52'). Populate the 'Descriptive Statistics' category with two True/False questions: Q4 ('The mean of a dataset is always equal to the median.' - False) and Q5 ('Standard deviation is always a non-negative value.' - True). Create a quiz named 'MATH201 Mid-Term Examination' in the MATH201 course configured with a 45-minute time limit, 1 maximum attempt, a passing grade of 60 out of 100, and enabled question shuffling. The quiz must include a random question section drawing 2 questions from 'Probability Basics' and another drawing 2 questions from 'Descriptive Statistics'. Login: admin / Admin1234!
